dependencies: Reliance on brokers and agents for business development
Dependence on ceding companies for accurate and timely loss reporting
Reliance on reinsurers to meet obligations under retrocessional agreements
Dependence on third-party investment managers for portfolio management
Reliance on information technology platform and third-party service providers
risks: Concentration risk with top brokers (Marsh and McLennan 21.9%, Aon 19.2% of gross written premiums)
Credit risk from reinsurers unable to meet obligations
Dependence on ceding companies for accurate data and timely reporting
Reliance on third-party claims service providers
Cybersecurity risks from third-party vendors and service providers
Operational risk from technology system failures or breaches

regulatory_risks: Insurance holding company regulations requiring prior approval for change of control
Dividend restrictions under Bermuda and U.S. state insurance laws
Capital adequacy requirements (Bermuda BSCR and U.S. NAIC RBC models)
Licensing requirements in multiple jurisdictions for insurance and reinsurance operations
Compliance with U.S., state and foreign insurance laws and regulations
Potential changes in U.S. federal oversight of insurance industry
European regulatory updates and new capital adequacy directives
Cybersecurity and data privacy regulations (NAIC Insurance Data Security Model Law, NY DFS cybersecurity regulation)
International tax law changes including OECD Base Erosion and Profit Shifting initiatives and Pillar Two 15% global minimum tax
U.S. tax law changes including Tax Cuts and Jobs Act provisions and potential future legislation
Bermuda Corporate Income Tax Act 2023 imposing 15% corporate income tax effective January 1, 2025
Potential U.S. taxation if Bermuda entities deemed engaged in U.S. trade or business
U.S. excise and withholding tax increases
Reinsurance premium excise tax of 1% on U.S. risks
Compliance with insurance laws in over 100 countries across six continents
Rate and policy form requirements in multiple U.S. states
Residual market participation requirements
Anti-takeover provisions in insurance laws
Foreign jurisdictions requiring reinsurer registration
Collateral requirements for unlicensed or non-admitted reinsurers
Potential increased supervision and regulation of reinsurers domiciled outside the U.S.

sanction_risks: Potential impact from international sanctions on business operations
Regulatory restrictions from domestic or foreign government measures
